response:
In C# it is possible to estimate the time it takes for a UDP packet to reach a server  
using the Stopwatch class  
you can send a packet and then wait for a response  
however UDP does not guarantee delivery or response  
so you must handle cases where no response is received  
you can calculate the time difference between sending and receiving  
but you must ensure the server is configured to respond  
you can use the Socket class to send and receive data  
you can start a Stopwatch before sending the packet  
and stop it when you receive a response  
this will give you the round trip time  
but you are only measuring one direction if you assume the response is immediate  
you must be aware that this method is not reliable  
as UDP does not provide built in timing features  
you can also use the Ping class but it measures round trip time  
not one direction only  
you can use the Send and Receive methods of the Socket class  
to send and receive data  
you can calculate the time difference between sending and receiving  
to estimate the time taken for the packet to reach the server  
you must handle exceptions and timeouts properly  
to ensure your application is robust and reliable
